contracts.shape: Optional top-level providers object keyed by a declared canonical provider prefix; each value is an object with optional base_url containing an absolute http:// or https:// URL. Unknown provider keys, relative URLs, userinfo, queries, fragments, and credential/capability/pricing fields are invalid. Resolution is config value then declared endpoint default. Request construction removes every trailing slash from the resolved base and appends exactly one operation path beginning with `/`; SWORN_<PROVIDER>_BASE_URL is warned about and ignored.
contracts.live_test: cmd/sworn/verify_test.go: subprocess CLI loads a temporary config.json override and sends a request to the configured real httptest HTTP transport
contracts.rationale: One public config shape must feed utility dispatch, loop dispatch, enumeration, and conformance without a second endpoint resolver. Credentials and capability data are explicitly excluded.

contracts.shape: Canonical Ollama Cloud bearer credential. Read from the process environment first and credentials.json second through model.ProviderKey("ollama-cloud"); never sent by keyless ollama/, llamacpp/, lmstudio/, vllm/, or localai/ descriptors and never logged.
contracts.live_test: internal/model/ollama_cloud_live_test.go: opt-in HTTPS Ollama Cloud request using OLLAMA_API_KEY with secret-safe output

contracts.shape: No Authorization header; short fixed timeout; no retries; available only for HTTP 2xx with a parseable OpenAI-compatible object containing a data array of model records. Error output names provider and endpoint but never response bodies or payloads.
contracts.edge_config: n/a: direct CLI request to an operator-configured endpoint; no repository-owned browser or proxy middleware

contracts.shape: Existing OpenAI-compatible chat request/response envelope used for ollama-cloud/, ollama/, llamacpp/, lmstudio/, vllm/, and localai/. Only descriptors declaring an API-key environment attach Authorization: Bearer; keyless local descriptors send no credential. Non-2xx, malformed, and empty responses fail closed without logging bodies.
contracts.live_test: internal/model/ollama_cloud_live_test.go: opt-in real Ollama Cloud chat completion plus internal/model/local_endpoint_live_test.go: opt-in local Ollama chat completion
contracts.edge_config: n/a: direct CLI request to provider HTTPS or an operator-configured local endpoint; no repository-owned browser middleware

contracts.shape: Authorization: Bearer <OLLAMA_API_KEY> over an https:// endpoint only. The header is absent for every keyless local descriptor. Logs and generated records may identify auth as required/present/absent but never include the credential value.
contracts.live_test: internal/model/ollama_cloud_live_test.go: opt-in real Ollama Cloud request succeeds while secret-safe assertions reject credential reflection
contracts.edge_config: n/a: direct CLI HTTPS request; provider-side edge configuration is outside this repository

contracts.shape: The probe action emits raw observation records with provider, probe_model, observed_at, classified probe results, and no payload bodies. The generate action emits a deterministic checked-in profiles object keyed by canonical endpoint prefix, containing only stable dialect request-shaping fields plus stable source-model identity; it strips observed_at and all capability observations. Runtime consumes only profiles. Generation sorts keys and strips secrets, request bodies, response bodies, and model payloads.
contracts.live_test: cmd/sworn/endpoint_conformance_test.go: subprocess CLI probes a real local HTTP transport, generates a normalized record, and the production dialect loader consumes it without mocks

contracts.shape: One row per declared OAI-compatible endpoint with provider, status RUN|FAIL|SKIP, reason code, probe-model identifier, and dialect-record result reference. Mandatory rows cannot be SKIP; zero executed mandatory rows is failure. No secrets or payload bodies are permitted.
contracts.rationale: The matrix makes unavailable providers explicit and machine-countable so a green workflow cannot hide unexecuted coverage.
